items.title: Hackers breach senior US Space Force official’s Instagram account and post Iranian propaganda - CNN
items.summary: # Hackers breach senior US Space Force official’s Instagram account and post Iranian propaganda. * Hackers breached the Instagram account of a senior Space Force official and posted pro-Iran propaganda. * Chief Master Sgt. John Bentivegna's account temporarily displayed videos invoking Vietnam War propaganda and anti-US messaging. Hackers breached a senior US Space Force official’s Instagram account and temporarily posted a string of pro-Iran and anti-US propaganda on Sunday, some of it invoking the Vietnam War. One video reviewed by CNN used audio from “Hanoi Hannah,” an infamous Vietnam War propagandist, telling US soldiers to “leave a sinking ship.” The video also featured images of late Iranian security official Ali Larijani, who was killed weeks into the US-Israel-Iran war. The hack of Bentivegna’s Instagram account is only the latest front in the propaganda battles that have been a feature of the US war with Iran.

items.title: China’s model smart dairy factory offers lessons for Vietnam - Báo VietNamNet
items.summary: ## Vietnam’s dairy manufacturers are pursuing digital transformation through open data platforms that connect aging machinery with modern IoT systems. In the fourth quarter of 2024, the World Economic Forum (WEF) officially recognized Mengniu’s Ningxia facility in China as a Lighthouse Factory. The Lighthouse Factory title is awarded by the WEF to manufacturing facilities that pioneer the use of advanced digital technologies to create customer value, accelerate time-to-market and optimize operational efficiency. The results achieved by the Lighthouse Factory model have established a new benchmark for the global dairy industry. Speaking to VietNamNet on the sidelines of the International Dairy and Dairy Products Exhibition 2026, Nguyen Van, Technical Director of Tetra Pak Vietnam, said the greatest challenge facing Vietnam’s dairy sector is the lack of infrastructure uniformity. In Vietnam, major dairy producers are beginning to incorporate these technologies into their smart factory roadmaps, gradually narrowing the gap between local manufacturing operations and the world’s leading Lighthouse Factories.

items.title: India confirms deal to supply Brahmos missile to Vietnam - Army Technology
items.summary: # India confirms deal to supply Brahmos missile to Vietnam. The two-stage missile has a flight range of up to 290km and is capable of carrying a conventional warhead weighing up to 200kg. India has signed an agreement to supply BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles to Vietnam, according to Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh. The potential deal with Vietnam was previously reported by Reuters, which cited two sources stating that Vietnamese President To Lam was expected to discuss expanding defence cooperation involving BrahMos missiles with India during a visit to New Delhi. India has previously exported BrahMos missiles to the Philippines and entered into a contract with Indonesia in March, as part of its efforts to increase domestic defence manufacturing for both domestic and export markets. Singh also stated that India is in the “final stages” of negotiations on a BrahMos missile supply deal with Indonesia. The design allows the missile to maintain supersonic speeds of up to 2.8 Mach throughout its flight, with a maximum range of 290km.

items.title: A Soviet Jew’s Warning About Propaganda - The Times of Israel
items.summary: * You will receive email alerts from this author. You will no longer receive email alerts from this author. # A Soviet Jew’s Warning About Propaganda. The idea was simple…have dialogue. For decades, universities have been the birthplace of intellectual movements and political activism. From Vietnam to civil rights to anti apartheid movements, college campuses have served as places where ideas are challenged, debated, and refined, and manifested. The goal was not to convince everyone to agree. The goal was to create an opportunity for students who had never met an Israeli soldier to engage with a real human being one-on-one. I grew up understanding the difference between media and propaganda. Propaganda is not designed to inform. Whatever one’s views of the war, no Israeli soldier is instructed to enter Gaza with the mission of killing children. How do we teach an entire generation to question what they are consuming rather than simply repeating it?

items.title: SEA-H2X cable linking China and Southeast Asia is ready for service - Developing Telecoms
items.summary: ## SEA-H2X cable linking China and Southeast Asia is ready for service. Chinese subsea cable firm HMN Tech has announced that the South East Asia Hainan–Hong Kong Express Cable System (SEA-H2X) connecting China to Southeast Asia is now ready for service. The 6,000-km SEA-H2X subsea cable – which is owned by China Mobile International, China Unicom Global and Converge ICT – runs from China’s Hainan Province and Hong Kong to the Philippines, Thailand and Singapore. It’s also designed to support extensions to Vietnam, Cambodia, Indonesia, East Malaysia and the Malaysian peninsula. HMN said in a statement on Thursday that it deployed its 39.5-nm bandwidth repeaters and ultra-low-loss optical fibres in the system, delivering a system capacity of over 200 Tbps designed to support the growing requirements of AI, cloud computing, and international data centres across Southeast Asia with higher capacity and lower latency. SEA-H2X has been in the works since at least 2022, and was initially scheduled to be ready for service in 2024.

items.title: Meet the top 10 most active defense tech VCs - PitchBook
items.summary: # Meet the top 10 most active defense tech VCs. A small group of venture capital firms is driving a record flood of capital into defense tech as investors seize geopolitical tailwinds, including the US war with Iran and continued tensions with China. VCs invested a record $19.8 billion into the sector in Q1—the most ever in a single quarter, and more than a third of 2025’s full-year total, according to PitchBook’s Q1 2026 Defense Tech VC Trends report. The most active check writers ranged from Andreessen Horowitz to a $50 million shop focused on deep tech, underscoring the widespread interest among VCs. Late-stage and venture growth rounds captured 88% of that $19.8 billion. The Iran war has been a significant factor in investment growth, but not the only one, according to Ryan Micheletti, co-founder and GP of The Veteran Fund. Here are the top 10 most-active VCs in defense tech in Q1 2026, ranked by deal count globally, according to PitchBook data.

items.title: 🚨A MARKETER QUIT HIS JOB, LEARNED AI, AND MADE $360,000 IN A SINGLE MONTH.
items.summary:
🚨A MARKETER QUIT HIS JOB, LEARNED AI, AND MADE $360,000 IN A SINGLE MONTH.

Not by trading manually.

Not by staring at charts all day.

He built an AI-powered BTC simulation engine using Claude.

And the results were wild.

A 35-year-old marketer from Hong Kong, known on Polymarket as "marketing101," went deep into AI and created a system that reportedly generated over $360K in profit.

The idea?

Instead of guessing where Bitcoin might go...

The system simulates thousands of possible market reactions before placing a trade.

What powers it:

→ Claude as the reasoning engine
→ MiroFish for force-graph simulations
→ Live market data from multiple exchanges
→ Order book analysis
→ Institutional flow signals
→ 10,000 simulation cycles before every trade

According to the breakdown, the bot:

✅ Pulls live BTC data from major exchanges
✅ Analyzes order book depth
✅ Simulates thousands of market outcomes
✅ Detects pricing inefficiencies
✅ Executes only when a strong statistical edge appears
✅ Adjusts position sizes automatically based on performance

What makes it different from typical retail trading bots?

→ Multi-simulation decision making
→ AI reasoning layer with Claude
→ Institutional-grade data sources
→ Risk management built into every trade

Risk controls include:

→ Half-Kelly position sizing
→ Daily loss caps
→ Auto-stop after consecutive losses
→ Paper trading before going live
→ No expensive GPU required

The recommended setup:

→ Claude API
→ MiroFish
→ Python environment
→ Trading wallet
→ Telegram alerts

The most interesting part?

He's not trying to predict the future.

He's running thousands of possible futures before the market moves.

Most traders watch charts.

AI traders are increasingly building systems that analyze probabilities at a scale humans can't.

Whether this specific strategy continues working or not...

The bigger lesson is clear:

AI is becoming a powerful tool for building automated decision systems.

Save this thread if you're interested in where AI and trading are heading next. 👇

items.title: San Francisco AI giant Anthropic files for IPO after $965 billion valuation - San Francisco Chronicle
items.summary: San Francisco AI giant Anthropic files for IPO after $965 billion valuation. Anthropic, the San Francisco artificial intelligence company behind the Claude chatbot, has confidentially filed paperwork for a proposed initial public offering, moving it closer to a possible Wall Street debut. The company said Monday it submitted a draft registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a proposed offering of common stock. The confidential filing does not guarantee Anthropic will go public, but it allows the company to begin the SEC review process before deciding whether to move ahead. The filing comes days after Anthropic announced a massive new funding round that valued the company at $965 billion. Anthropic is one of the Bay Area’s most prominent AI companies, along with OpenAI. Its growth has been driven in part by demand for Claude and AI tools used by businesses and software developers. Aidin Vaziri is a staff writer at The San Francisco Chronicle.

items.title: Anthropic races toward a Wall Street debut with a confidential SEC filing - Greenwich Time
items.summary: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic is moving toward going public on Wall Street, the latest chapter in its meteoric rise from a little-known research laboratory to one of the leading AI companies valued at $965 billion. Anthropic said Monday it has submitted a confidential filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a proposed initial public offering of its common stock. “This gives us the option to go public after the SEC completes its review,” Anthropic said in a brief statement. The company said it hasn't decided on the number or price of shares to be offered. Anthropic said last week it had raised $65 billion in private funding that will push its valuation to $965 billion, a whopping number that makes the five-year-old maker of the Claude chatbot one of the world’s most valuable startups. items.title: VC Bill Gurley says Anthropic is 'midwifing a deity' by 'building a species that's superior to humans' - Business Insider
items.summary: # VC Bill Gurley says Anthropic is 'midwifing a deity' by 'building a species that's superior to humans'. "The more I dig, I've met people who I dare say think it's their responsibility and they're excited about building a species that's superior to humans," Gurley said during a recent episode of the "All-In Podcast.". Gurley, who said he spent a month reading everything he could about Anthropic, said he came away convinced that one of the leading AI startups wants to create a God-like system to rule humanity. I think they're midwifing a deity here," Gurley said. "He says, 'It could be a capitalist economy of AI systems which then give out resources to humans based on some secondary economy of what the AI systems think makes sense to reward in humans.' So that's envisioning a deity of sorts that's going to break ties and decide what humans do," Gurley said, quoting from the essay.

items.title: Anthropic files for IPO before OpenAI as trillion-dollar startups race to go public - NBC News
items.summary: # Anthropic files for IPO before OpenAI amid race to the public markets for trillion-dollar startups. The move comes earlier than expected as the Claude AI maker looks to beat its chief rival to fresh funding. Anthropic has filed for an initial public stock offering, a surprise start to its race with OpenAI to go public.Chris Ratcliffe / Bloomberg via Getty Images file. Artificial intelligence startup Anthropic said Monday it filed for an initial public stock offering, a surprise start to the race between it and OpenAI to be the next trillion-dollar AI startup to hit the public markets. The filing, which is currently confidential as the Securities and Exchange Commission reviews it, comes earlier than expected as the Claude AI maker looks to beat its primary rival to fresh funding. Beating OpenAI to the market will likely be important for Anthropic, because its trading debut is set to occur shortly after SpaceX’s trillion-dollar IPO.

items.title: And it's not just Google.
items.summary:
And it's not just Google.

· Microsoft on OpenAI: $228B on $13.8B (~17.6x)
· Amazon on Anthropic: ~$74B on $8B (~9x)
· Nvidia on CoreWeave alone: ~$3.66B on the early checks, plus locked-in GPU revenue

Top-decile VC funds do 3-5x. Big Tech strategic books are doing 10-20x — and binding the customer at the same time.

The new playbook: capital + compute + customer, all from one balance sheet.

items.title: 这两天 Anthropic 修了 Claude Code 一个 bug：它会自己 spawn 一堆 subagent，把额度飞快烧光。
items.summary:
这两天 Anthropic 修了 Claude Code 一个 bug：它会自己 spawn 一堆 subagent，把额度飞快烧光。

我让cc用agentlog数了下：554 个 subagent，一共烧了 580 万 token。有一次它自己开了 75 个 subagent，另一次 66 个烧掉 83 万。

真正贵的是两处：一是偶尔冒一个失控的，我这边最猛的一个 subagent 单独吃了 21 万 token；二是图省事全用 general-purpose 这种万能型，平均一个一万二，其实大部分活派给轻一点的专用 agent（探查的、审查的）就够，一个才四到八千。

所以还是别让它无脑铺 subagent，小范围的活就用小范围的 agent。
